title: ischaemum aristatum subsp. barbatum
intro: Ischaemum aristatum subsp. barbatum, also known as the Wild Rye or Barbed Ischaemum, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties. The health benefits of this plant include relief from fever, inflammation, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and reduce stress. Ischaemum aristatum subsp. barbatum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for its medicinal purposes, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
